Special cases
-In this version of poker, a pair is<FF03>not a winning hand!
-In a straight, an ace is not lower<FF03>than a 2!
-The Joker is a wild card that can be<FF03>used as part of any winning hand!
It's a video blackjack machine!
This is a one-on-one-game of blackjack<FF03>against the dealer!
The computer acts as the dealer!
Two cards are dealt to the player and<FF03>the dealer at the outset!
One of the dealer's two cards will be<FF03>kept face-down!
If the dealer's shown card is an ace,<FF03>you may place an insurance bet!
The insurance mechanic will be explained<FF03>at the end of these rules!
First, compare your cards to the dealer's<FF03>to decide whether to draw another card!
If you decide to draw, select Hit!<FF03>If not, select Stand!
You may draw up to 7 cards as long as<FF03>their total value remains 21 or lower!
The following circumstances will result<FF03>in an automatic Stand
-Your initial draw is blackjack!
-The total of the cards drawn after<FF03>Hitting is 21!
-You draw seven cards!
-The cards' total value surpasses 21,<FF03>resulting in a bust!
2 through 10 are face value! J through<FF03>K are worth 10, and A is worth either<FF03>1 or 11!
After the player Stands, the dealer<FF03>will draw cards!

It's a slot machine!
This slot machine consists of 5<FF03>big reels and 16 small reels!
The number of ways to line up a win<FF03>increases with the size of your bet!
You can bet up to 8 coins!
Bets 1, 2, 5, and 6 are for the<FF03>horizontal lines,while 3, 4, 7,<FF03>and 8 are for the vertical lines!
Bets 5 through 8 also add two ways<FF03>to line up a win per bet!
When the game begins, the big and small<FF03>reels will all start to spin!
Once the reels have spun enough,<FF03>the small reels will stop!
The big<FF03>reels will continue to spin!
Press the
, and
buttons to<FF03>rotate the corresponding reels!
Adjust the small reels so that it will<FF03>be easier to line up a pattern!
When you have finished, press the<FF03>R button to stop the big reels!
If three or more of a symbol are lined<FF03>up along one of your bets, you win!
Up to five symbols can cross bets 1<FF03>through 4, and up to four can cross<FF03>bets 5 through 8!
When the symbols on the big reels match<FF03>diagonally, in all four corners, or all<FF03>five at once
You win regardless of which lines you<FF03>bet on!
Earnings for hits on the big reels<FF03>increase according to the number of<FF03>bets you made!

Different slot machines have different<FF03>patterns and odds!
It's best to try out different machines<FF03>rather than concentrating on one!
Be careful not to forget to rotate the<FF03>small reels during the game!
Careful adjustment of the small reels<FF03>is key to maximizing your payout!
If you begin the game with fewer bets,<FF03>you may face greater losses when it<FF03>matters most!
For best results, always play the game<FF03>with the full eight bets!
It's a video dice game!
In this game, two dice are rolled on<FF03>screen, and you predict the outcome!
You can bet on eleven rolls, from 2<FF03>to 12, and you may bet on as many rolls<FF03>per game as you wish!
However, you may bet a maximum of 100<FF03>coins on any given roll!
If you bet on the correct roll, a<FF03>multiplier determines your winnings!
You can also stake your winnings on<FF03>an Extra Game!
In the Extra Game, you do not predict<FF03>the outcome of the roll
<FF03>Instead, any roll other than 7 wins!
The amount you win differs depending on<FF03>the roll!
If the roll is 7, the Extra Game ends<FF03>and all your winnings are lost, so be<FF03>careful!

The closer the roll is to 7, the lower<FF03>the multiplier! This is because rolls<FF03>close to 7 appear more frequently!
Thus, the safe bet is on rolls close<FF03>to 7, with rolls like 2 and 12 being<FF03>riskier!
In the Extra Game, if a roll of 7<FF03>comes up, you lose all your winnings!
The trick to not losing too much is<FF03>not to get greedy and leave with a<FF03>reasonable amount!
Metal Cards!
It's a Code Breaker machine!
In this game, the numbers 1 through 9<FF03>are dispersed throughout a grid!
Your goal is to guess the arrangement<FF03>of the numbers!
The fewer tries it takes, the better<FF03>the prize you receive!
If you cannot crack it in 8 tries,<FF03>the game ends!
The Result to the left of the screen<FF03>shows your previous attempts!
If you guess incorrectly, the guess<FF03>will be displayed in the Results!
Use it as a reference when formulating<FF03>your next guess!
The Chosen display at the top shows<FF03>the numbers in use!
All numbers, 1 through 9, must be used<FF03>in the code!
Use the Chosen display to see which<FF03>numbers are missing from your guess!
